problem:
At the mall's food court, Crystal wants to buy a meal consisting of one entree, one drink and one dessert. The table below lists Crystal's favorite foods in the food court. How many distinct possible meals can she buy from these options?

\begin{tabular}{ |c | c | c | }
\hline \textbf{Entrees} & \textbf{Drinks}&\textbf{Desserts} \\ \hline
Pizza & Lemonade & Frozen Yogurt \\ \hline
Chicken Teriyaki & Root Beer & Chocolate Chip Cookie \\ \hline
Corn Dog& & \\ \hline
Fish and Chips& & \\ \hline
\end{tabular}

classified_questions.question: What are the different components that make up a meal for Crystal in the food court?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many options does Crystal have for selecting an entree?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: After selecting an entree, how many options does Crystal have for selecting a drink?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Once Crystal has chosen an entree and a drink, how many options does she have for selecting a dessert?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we find the total number of possible meals that Crystal can buy by considering the number of choices for each component?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain how we arrived at the final answer of 16 possible meals?
classified_questions.label: b
